name: Mancha Manteles
recipeIngredient: Tomatoes, ½ lb, peeled, seeded and chopped
Cooking apples, firm, peeled and sliced, 2
recipeInstructions:
This recipe closely resembles Mancha Manteles, as described by Richard Condon in The Mexican Stove. It consists of the following ingredients: one turkey (or two whole chickens), four chorizo sausages, sliced pineapples, sliced apples, sliced bananas, ancho, pasilla, and serrano chiles, almonds, cinnamon, lard, tomato puree.
1. Brown the turkey in lard until fully cooked. Remove it and set aside, leaving 2 tablespoons of fat in the pan.
2. In the same pan, fry the chiles, almonds, and sesame seeds together until fragrant.
3. Blend all ingredients into a smooth puree using a blender.
4. Add the chorizo meat to the mixture and cook until browned, then remove it.
5. In the remaining fat, sauté the onions until soft.
6. Mix in the blended mixture, tomatoes, and cinnamon.
7. Cook for a few minutes before transferring everything into a large casserole dish.
8. Stir in the stock, sugar, cider vinegar, and salt to taste.
9. Return the turkey pieces, chorizo meat, and fruit to the dish.
10. Cover and simmer for 45 minutes to 1 hour until fully cooked and flavors are well combined.
